name: This Museum Is Not Obsolete
content:
I’ve been keeping up with Look Mum No Computer’s work [https://www.lookmumnocomputer.com/] since around 2018. When he originally announced This Museum Is Not Obsolete, I resigned myself to thinking I would never get a chance to visit the museum. When I found out airplane tickets between Ireland and London can be purchased for under 20 Euro, I planned my entire first trip to England just as an excuse to go visit this museum.
The entire museum is the size of several large closets linked together, but it is jam packed from floor to ceiling with musical gear, old computers, and every other doohicky imaginable, all free for you to mess around with. I entirely lost track of time while in the museum, assuming that I would be gently guided towards the exit when closing time came. Instead, I got to hang out with Sam (Look Mum No Computer) and the rest of the museum crew for half an hour after closing, until I realized that I should probably buy my merch and stop bothering the museum folks.
People say that you shouldn’t meet your idols, but if your idol is a museum, then it turns out it is perfectly alright and you should absolutely go visit it. The hours are a little limited in the winter, though I think the hours are more accommodating in the more touristy summer months. It turns out Sam is shorter than I expected? I didn’t even notice him walk into the museum until he was standing next to me and I had been chatting with him for a bit. Super cool guy, I might’ve gotten a little too nervous to talk with him once I did realize who he was.
